description: Known HTML/CSS/JS anti-patterns for asym-intel.info. Every Computer session reads this at Step 0. When a new error is discovered in any session, append it here before the session ends.

anti_patterns.description: Inline padding-top on body instead of using base.css
anti_patterns.correct: base.css owns body { padding-top: 40px }. Never add inline or in a <style> block.
anti_patterns.root_cause: Agent regenerating what base.css already provides

anti_patterns.description: nav.js loaded at bottom of <body> instead of <head>
anti_patterns.root_cause: Causes network bar not appearing on first paint — visible as layout flash on mobile

anti_patterns.description: Raw --monitor-accent used on light surface without color-mix()
anti_patterns.root_cause: Fails WCAG AA contrast ratio on white for 5 of 7 monitors (WDM/GMM/FCW/ESA/ERM)

anti_patterns.description: Component styles placed in monitor.css instead of base.css
anti_patterns.correct: monitor.css is MAX 40 lines, accent CSS custom properties only. All components live in base.css.
anti_patterns.root_cause: Agent writing scoped styles rather than consulting base.css — creates 7x maintenance burden

anti_patterns.description: overflow:hidden on layout container breaking position:sticky
anti_patterns.root_cause: overflow:hidden creates a scroll container that silently breaks all sticky children

anti_patterns.description: Hardcoded font-size below --text-min token
anti_patterns.correct: font-size: var(--text-min)  — never go below this token (clamp(0.8125rem,...))
anti_patterns.root_cause: Produces illegible text on mobile — not caught until viewport testing

anti_patterns.description: Inline <nav data-asym-network-bar> HTML written into page source
anti_patterns.correct: nav.js injects the network bar dynamically at runtime. Never write it inline.
anti_patterns.root_cause: Agent copying bar structure instead of letting nav.js own injection — produces duplicate bar

anti_patterns.description: Custom HTML-escaping function defined inline instead of using shared esc()
anti_patterns.correct: Use esc(str) or escHtml(str) from renderer.js — already imported on every monitor page
anti_patterns.root_cause: Agent unaware of shared renderer utilities — creates divergent escaping logic

anti_patterns.description: background property set on .signal-block in monitor.css
anti_patterns.correct: base.css owns .signal-block background with !important. monitor.css may only set border, border-radius, font-family.
anti_patterns.root_cause: Override silently defeated by !important in base.css — causing confusion and no visual effect
anti_patterns.real_world_case: ESA monitor.css had linear-gradient override; SCEM had rgba(0.06) override — both defeated silently

anti_patterns.description: Chart.js CDN loaded after charts.js in <head>
anti_patterns.correct: Chart.js CDN MUST appear before charts.js in <head>
anti_patterns.root_cause: charts.js depends on Chart being defined at parse time — wrong order causes silent failure

anti_patterns.description: innerHTML set to return value of void function (produces literal 'undefined')
anti_patterns.wrong: element.innerHTML = AsymPersistent.renderEntityList(items)  when function returns void
anti_patterns.correct: Check whether the renderer function returns a string or writes to DOM directly. Call accordingly.
anti_patterns.root_cause: AGM persistent.html: M07, M15 sections all set innerHTML to undefined — rendered as literal string
anti_patterns.real_world_case: AGM persistent.html M07 risk vectors, M15 AISI pipeline, M15 lab postures — fixed PR #20 2026-04-01

anti_patterns.description: Variable defined in one IIFE/script block referenced from another
anti_patterns.wrong: Script block 1: (function(){ var KEYS = [...] })()  —  Script block 2: KEYS.forEach(...)
anti_patterns.correct: Variables used across script blocks must be defined in outer scope or re-declared in each block
anti_patterns.root_cause: SCEM dashboard: INDICATOR_KEYS defined inside IIFE in block 1; renderEscalationGauge() in block 2 referenced it — ReferenceError
anti_patterns.real_world_case: SCEM dashboard.html INDICATOR_KEYS scope error — fixed session 2026-04-01

anti_patterns.description: Cron prompt update triggers off-schedule publish (guards bypassed)
anti_patterns.wrong: Cron agent runs full pipeline when prompt file is updated mid-week
anti_patterns.correct: Publish guard (day + hour + recency) must be the FIRST executed block. Hard exit if any condition fails. Explicitly state: 'A prompt reload is NOT a reason to publish.'
anti_patterns.root_cause: WDM/GMM/FCW/ESA/AGM all published mid-week on 2026-04-01 after prompt updates. Recency guard was present but not enforced as first action.
anti_patterns.real_world_case: 5 monitors published out-of-schedule on 2026-04-01 — hardened guard applied to all 7 prompts

anti_patterns.description: JSON output truncated silently — deep sections never written
anti_patterns.wrong: Cron agent produces one commit covering all sections — deep sections dropped when output is too long
anti_patterns.correct: Use two-pass commit pattern: Pass 1 = core sections, Pass 2 = deep sections via read-modify-PUT. Verify all keys present before Step 3.
anti_patterns.root_cause: WDM Category B sections (electoral_watch, digital_civil, etc.) missing from all 3 issues — cron hit output limit
anti_patterns.real_world_case: WDM Issues 1-3 missing 8 Category B sections. Two-pass rule applied to all 7 monitors 2026-04-01.

anti_patterns.description: source_url field present but always empty string
anti_patterns.correct: Every heatmap entry, indicator, and intelligence item must have a non-empty source_url pointing to a Tier 1 or Tier 2 primary source
anti_patterns.root_cause: WDM heatmap: all 29 entries had source_url = '' across Issues 1-3. Credibility gap for researchers.

anti_patterns.description: Country name displayed without flag emoji where AsymRenderer.flag() is available
anti_patterns.root_cause: AsymRenderer.flag(iso2) is available on every monitor page via renderer.js. Always use it wherever a country name or actor code appears in a table, chart label, card, or timeline.
anti_patterns.applies_to: All monitor pages — tables, charts, timelines, cards, delta strips, actor boards. Wherever a country name or ISO-2 actor code appears.
anti_patterns.real_world_case: FCW campaign timeline actor group labels showed 'RU', 'CN' etc with no flags. Actor profiles (same page) already used AsymRenderer.flag correctly — inconsistency.

anti_patterns.description: Raw --monitor-accent used as text colour on tinted panel background (monitor-accent-bg)
anti_patterns.root_cause: var(--monitor-accent-bg) is a light tinted surface (~12% opacity). Raw --monitor-accent fails WCAG AA contrast on it for 5 of 7 monitors. The 65% darkened mix gives minimum 4.8:1 contrast.
anti_patterns.applies_to: All JS-generated elements with tinted backgrounds: badges, risk-heat cells, tag chips, timeline labels, actor boards, delta strip items, campaign cards.
anti_patterns.scope_note: This violation occurs almost entirely in JavaScript-generated innerHTML strings — not detectable by the static validator (Check 20). Must be caught by code review and anti-patterns awareness.

anti_patterns.description: Signal block panel does not link to the primary source story
anti_patterns.wrong: Signal block shows headline and summary with no link
anti_patterns.correct:
Always render a 'Read the top story ↓' link in every signal block where source_url is available. Pattern: (sourceUrl ? '
Read the top story ↓
' : '')
anti_patterns.applies_to: All monitor dashboard signal/lead-signal blocks. Every signal block that references a specific story or development must link to it. The source_url is always present in report-latest.json signal object.
anti_patterns.rationale: Users reading 'Confirmed Lead Signal: Hungary 11 days...' have no way to follow up without navigating manually. The source_url is collected every issue — surfacing it is a one-line fix.
anti_patterns.real_world_case: FCW, WDM dashboards had no source link on signal block. ESA had 'Source →'. SCEM had 'Primary source →'. Standardised to 'Read the top story ↓' across all 7 monitors.

anti_patterns.description: Hugo brief filename must match front-matter date. Filename slug determines the public URL — a filename of 2026-04-02-weekly-brief.md produces a future-dated URL even if the front matter says date: 2026-04-01. Always set filename = YYYY-MM-DD matching the date: field in front matter exactly.
anti_patterns.why: Future-dated URLs appear in sitemap.xml and can cause Google Search Console to reject the sitemap as containing invalid entries. Dates must be consistent.
anti_patterns.applies_to: all cron agents writing Hugo briefs

anti_patterns.description: Plain text escaping (escHtml/innerHTML with escHtml) applied to content fields that contain markdown or HTML — produces literal ** and <a> text in the rendered page
anti_patterns.wrong:
briefEl.innerHTML = '

' + escHtml(d.weekly_brief) + '

' → renders '**bold**' and '
anti_patterns.correct: Use a safe renderer that splits on ... tags (pass through), escapes only the text nodes, then applies **bold** → transform. Never run escHtml() over a field that may contain markdown or HTML markup.
anti_patterns.root_cause: weekly_brief and similar narrative fields are generated by cron agents using markdown syntax (**bold**, numbered lists) and raw HTML anchor tags. Treating them as plain text destroys the formatting.
anti_patterns.applies_to: Any data field that may contain markdown or HTML: weekly_brief, note, summary fields generated by LLMs, narrative sections. Check the actual data content before choosing renderer.
anti_patterns.real_world_case: WDM dashboard weekly_brief section showed '**1. HUNGARY — 11 DAYS...**' literally instead of bold heading. Fixed with safe renderer that splits on <a> tags and applies bold transform. 2026-04-02.
anti_patterns.safe_renderer_pattern: Split text on (]+>.*?) — odd-indexed parts are anchor tags (pass through), even-indexed parts are text nodes (escHtml + markdown transforms).
